entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Students read about the story of cartography from ancient Mesopotamia up through the discovery of Antarctica in the late 1800s and draw maps along the way. The course finishes with a term of historical geography where students follow the steps of Canadian explorers from the 19th century and learn about Canadian provinces through map work.

Geography: Grade 7 (Canada)
The book used in Term 3—Exploring the Frozen North by Pierre Berton—is a compendium of several shorter books written for younger readers by Berton on the theme of Arctic exploration. One such book is assigned for the course: Trapped in the Arctic. This collection, however, was the most cost-effective and accessible way to get the book. The other selections would make interesting free reads or read-alouds, but because there is some sensitive content in the other books, parents may prefer to preread.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: The first two terms are the same every year and shared between both US and Canadian courses. Term 3 brings in resources from the historical time period and is specific to Canadian students.

rows.label: Geography: Grade 7 (Canada)
rows.books: Mapping the World with Art, Canadian Student Atlas, Exploring the Frozen North
